name: Clapper Creator Monetization
reason: TikTok alternative that pays creators through livestream gifts (converts to cash), Fam subscriptions, short-form video gifts, and paid events. No follower minimum to start earning. Creators reported earning up to $3,000/month. Active in 2025.
payoutModel: Virtual gifts → diamonds → cash; subscriptions (Fam Tiers); video gifts; paid events. Min $100 withdrawal via bank/PayPal.
notes: No follower minimum to monetize (removed 1,000-follower Fam requirement in 2023). Creator-first algorithm gives all content equal exposure. Strong alternative for TikTok creators.

name: Bigo Live Official Host Program
reason: Live streaming platform paying creators via virtual gifts (diamonds → beans → cash) plus guaranteed monthly salary for Official Hosts. Very detailed payout structure verified. Active and expanding in 2025.
payoutModel: Viewers buy diamonds → send gifts → convert to beans (210 beans = $1 USD). Official Hosts get guaranteed monthly salary + gift earnings. Min withdrawal: 6,700 beans (~$31.90).
notes: Official Host requires 30-35 streaming hours/month across 15 days, min 40,000 beans target. 3-month auto-renewing contracts via agency. Weekly max withdrawal: 1,050,000 beans ($5,000). Top streamers reportedly earn $34,000–$68,000/month. Payment via bank transfer or Payoneer.

name: Tango Live Streaming
reason: Live streaming platform paying via virtual diamonds convertible to cash. 5,000 diamonds = $25 USD. Active payout system with multiple withdrawal methods including PayPal and crypto. Verified active in 2025.
payoutModel: Viewers send virtual gifts → converted to diamonds. 5,000 diamonds = $25 USD. VIP creators also get weekly bonuses and manager support. Min first withdrawal $15, then $25.
notes: Also offers Premium Match Calls (pay-per-minute video calls) and social feed earning. Daily withdrawal max 400,000 diamonds ($2,000). Payment via Mastercard Virtual Card, VISA, Paxum, Airwallex, PayPal, crypto. One creator earned $284 in first month streaming ~30 min/day.

name: Fanbase Creator Platform
reason: Black-owned social media platform that pays creators 70% on subscriptions and allows monetization of videos, photos, audio via 'Love' (digital tips). Major 2025 updates including lowered $5 minimum cashout threshold. Active and growing.
payoutModel: 70% revenue share on subscriptions ($2.99–$99.99/month). 'Love' digital currency tips (each Love = ~$0.005 to creator). Payouts monthly via PayPal and Stripe. Min cashout reduced to $5 in Feb 2025.
notes: Founded by Isaac Hayes III, specifically designed to combat racial pay disparities for Black creators. Supports short-form videos (Flickz), long-form (Fanbase+), stories, live streams, audio rooms. Equity crowdfunding allows creators to invest in platform. New discovery algorithm rolled out 2025.

name: Stageit Live Performance Platform
reason: Online live concert platform where musicians/performers host ticketed shows and earn 73% of all ticket sales and tips. Completely open — any artist can sign up and start performing. Active for 10+ years with millions paid out.
payoutModel: Artists set their own ticket prices (min $2.50). Earn 73% of tickets + tips. Stageit covers bandwidth, servers, transaction fees, and PRO blanket license (cover songs allowed). Payout via PayPal or check.
notes: Min cashout 250 Notes ($25). Payments processed within 10 business days. Acquired by VNUE in 2021. Free to use for performers. Covers legal cover song licensing — a major differentiator. Great for musicians looking for direct fan monetization.

name: LinkedIn BrandLink Video Program
reason: LinkedIn launched BrandLink in June 2025 — ad revenue sharing program placing brand video ads alongside premium B2B creator content (similar to YouTube pre-roll). Pays creators a share of ad revenue from their video content. Active as of 2025.
payoutModel: Ad revenue share from video ads (pre-roll style) placed on creator content. Initial cohort ~30 influencers and 13 publishers. Revenue split not publicly disclosed but previous publisher models suggest ~50% to creator.
notes: Launched June 2, 2025. Initially B2B influencers and publishers. LinkedIn video watch time up 36% YoY driving this launch. Separate from LinkedIn Learning (which is already in DB). Expanding beyond initial cohort.

name: Facebook Content Monetization Program
reason: Meta's unified Content Monetization Program (CMP) launched August 31, 2025, consolidating all video creator earnings (In-Stream Ads, Reels Ads, Performance Bonuses) into one program. Pays for short and long-form video. Different from the existing facebook-creator-fast-track in DB.
payoutModel: Ad revenue share based on 'Qualified Views' and engagement. 60% of payouts go to Reels in 2025. New 'Earnings Rate' metric = approx earnings per 1,000 qualified views. Requires Professional Mode or Creator Page.
notes: Replaced In-Stream Ads, Reels Ads, and Performance Bonuses after Aug 31, 2025. Facebook paid creators nearly $3 billion in 2025 (35% increase YoY). Separate from facebook-creator-fast-track which is an onboarding incentive. Enable via Creator Dashboard.

name: Cameo Creator Program
reason: Creators make personalized video messages for fans at prices they set. Creators keep ~75% of each booking. Active, well-established marketplace for video shoutouts, advice, and live calls. Open to apply.
payoutModel: Creators set their own price per video/live call. Keep 75% of earnings per booking. Weekly payouts via direct deposit, PayPal, or Venmo.
notes: Not just for celebrities — anyone can apply. Niches include athletes, musicians, influencers, comedians, gamers. Also offers Cameo Live (live video calls with fans). Weekly payout schedule. Very low barrier to entry.
